summary.intent: Fix a security bug in moltis where the exec allowlist was not enforced when approval_mode was set to 'never' (Off), allowing any command to proceed even when a non-empty allowlist was configured.
summary.outcome: Implemented and committed a targeted fix in approval.rs that enforces the allowlist under ApprovalMode::Off when a non-empty allowlist is configured, added 5 regression tests, updated the config template comment, and verified all 33 tests pass with clean clippy output.

repo: ApprovalMode::Off bypasses allowlist enforcement entirely — the allowlist was only consulted in the OnMiss branch, meaning security_level=allowlist + approval_mode=never had no enforcement.
SecurityLevel::Full early-returns Proceed before the mode match (approval.rs:285), so Full semantics are unaffected by changes to the Off arm.
The dangerous-pattern regex check at lines 272–279 fires before the mode match and returns NeedsApproval even in Off mode — this can hang headless agents.
The project uses a beads issue tracker; issues were created and claimed before implementation started.
The just lint recipe pulls in llama-cpp-sys-2 which has a pre-existing CMake/CUDA build failure unrelated to Rust code changes.

code.finding: check_command short-circuits to Proceed in ApprovalMode::Off without consulting self.allowlist; fix adds allowlist enforcement here when allowlist is non-empty.
Dangerous-pattern check runs before mode match and returns NeedsApproval even in Off mode, which hangs headless agents — known issue, deferred as follow-up.
SecurityLevel::Full early-returns Proceed here, before the ApprovalMode match, preserving full-mode semantics regardless of allowlist.
Existing test_approval_off_mode uses an empty allowlist, which is why it continued passing after the fix — empty allowlist preserves old unrestricted behavior.
exec config template updated to note that allowlist is now enforced under approval_mode=never when non-empty.

workflow: Running clippy narrowly on the touched crate (cargo clippy -p moltis-tools) is a reliable workaround when just lint fails due to unrelated native build issues.
Commit message followed fix(scope): format with Fixes #issue reference in body and no Co-Authored-By trailer.
Beads issue was created and claimed before implementation, then closed at commit time.

friction: just lint failed due to a pre-existing CMake/CUDA build failure in llama-cpp-sys-2, requiring a narrow per-crate clippy invocation as a workaround.
open_items: Dangerous-pattern NeedsApproval hanging in headless Off mode should deny instead of hang — deferred as follow-up issue.
Config validation warning when approval_mode=never + empty allowlist + security_level=allowlist (issue's Option 3) was deferred as a follow-up.
Docs page for exec approval (docs/src/) was conditionally skipped — should be revisited if a relevant page exists.
